question: What is the maximum Earned Leave carry-forward balance?
clean_answer: A maximum of 45 days of Earned Leave may be carried forward at the end of each financial year. Any balance exceeding this limit will be automatically encashed.
answer_with_sources: A maximum of 45 days of Earned Leave may be carried forward at the end of each financial year. Any balance exceeding this limit will be automatically encashed.
reference_answer: A maximum of 45 Earned Leave days may be carried forward. Any excess is automatically encashed at the employee's basic daily rate and credited in the April payroll.

question: Who is eligible for Hybrid WFH?
clean_answer: Yes, employees at grade L3 and above are eligible for Hybrid WFH, with a maximum of 3 days per week, provided they have a reliable internet connection and a dedicated workspace, and their reporting manager agrees to the arrangement in writing.
answer_with_sources: Yes, employees at grade L3 and above are eligible for Hybrid WFH, with a maximum of 3 days per week, provided they have a reliable internet connection and a dedicated workspace, and their reporting manager agrees to the arrangement in writing.
reference_answer: Hybrid WFH is available to eligible permanent employees at grade L3 and above, subject to the policy criteria and manager agreement.

question: When is an employee placed on a PIP?
clean_answer: An employee is placed on a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) when they receive a rating of 1 or 2 in two consecutive review cycles. The PIP is a structured and time-bound programme aimed at giving the employee a fair opportunity to improve. The PIP duration is 60 to 90 days, as determined by the reporting manager and HR Business Partner.
answer_with_sources: An employee is placed on a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) when they receive a rating of 1 or 2 in two consecutive review cycles. The PIP is a structured and time-bound programme aimed at giving the employee a fair opportunity to improve. The PIP duration is 60 to 90 days, as determined by the reporting manager and HR Business Partner.
reference_answer: An employee who receives a rating of 1 or 2 in two consecutive review cycles is placed on a formal Performance Improvement Plan.
